current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: Use the current best working JumpReLU K=128 setup as the stability anchor and make one minor parameter adjustment by testing AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 in proxy.
Switch from stalled JumpReLU tuning to a proxy top-k architecture run at K=128 while keeping the strongest validated training settings fixed for a clean architecture comparison.
Launch a proxy gated-family prototype at K=128 using the strongest validated training regime, but avoid architecture-specific override keys that previously caused invalid-env crashes.

recent_round_summaries.hypothesis: Returning to the best known working JumpReLU K=128 configuration and modestly increasing AUXK_ALPHA to 0.0625 may improve training stability and feature health without changing the core architecture or optimization regime, potentially recovering some proxy quality after the recent stalled sweeps.

JumpReLU local search appears exhausted, and reverting to the fundamentally different plain top-k SAE may provide a cleaner reconstruction-support tradeoff at K=128 under the same strong optimization settings, serving as a robust architecture reset after the failed gated launch attempts.

Top-k was only modestly competitive and still far from the K=128 quality target, so the next fundamentally different probe is the gated SAE family using only launcher-safe overrides; separating support selection from magnitude may recover cleaner latent routing than both JumpReLU and plain top-k.

gated.design_hypothesis: Top-k was only modestly competitive and still far from the K=128 quality target, so the next fundamentally different probe is the gated SAE family using only launcher-safe overrides; separating support selection from magnitude may recover cleaner latent routing than both JumpReLU and plain top-k.
next_steps: If gated runs cleanly and improves proxy FVU, stabilize the family with a follow-up that tunes gate-specific parameters or promotes to full if the gain is large enough.
If gated runs cleanly but is still uncompetitive, conclude that the current non-code architecture set is exhausted and plan a code-backed new family next.

If gated still crashes under launcher-safe overrides, retire it as an execution-layer mismatch and focus future architecture exploration on families that can be expressed with the current valid override surface.

topk.design_hypothesis: JumpReLU local search appears exhausted, and reverting to the fundamentally different plain top-k SAE may provide a cleaner reconstruction-support tradeoff at K=128 under the same strong optimization settings, serving as a robust architecture reset after the failed gated launch attempts.
next_steps: If top-k improves proxy FVU, promote to full and then stabilize the top-k family before revisiting cost or smaller-K tradeoffs.
If top-k is competitive but not better, compare its runtime and stability against JumpReLU to decide whether it is a better base for further architecture work.
If top-k is clearly worse, conclude that a stronger new family is needed and plan a code-backed architecture exploration round rather than more JumpReLU-local parameter sweeps.

recent_performance_findings: round 3: suspected performance regression for jumprelu (tps=16601.650675, baseline_ratio=0.200000, termination=throughput_too_low)
next_move_guidance: If gated runs cleanly and improves proxy FVU, stabilize the family with a follow-up that tunes gate-specific parameters or promotes to full if the gain is large enough.
If gated runs cleanly but is still uncompetitive, conclude that the current non-code architecture set is exhausted and plan a code-backed new family next.

If gated still crashes under launcher-safe overrides, retire it as an execution-layer mismatch and focus future architecture exploration on families that can be expressed with the current valid override surface.
